events.title: Barge Cibro Savannah explosion and fuel oil release into the Arthur Kill near Linden, New Jersey
events.description: The barge Cibro Savannah exploded while departing a dock near Linden, New Jersey, prompting an emergency response in the Arthur Kill waterway. NOAA incident reporting and contemporaneous news accounts described a release of No. 2 fuel oil from the barge and ongoing monitoring and response actions.

location.name: Arthur Kill waterway near Linden, New Jersey, USA

sources.headline: Oil barge blows up, burns in NY-NJ area wracked by spills

sources.source_name: NOAA Office of Response and Restoration
sources.headline: 30 years ago: Oil Pollution Act of 1990

key_quotes.quote: Incident reporting indicates a loss of about 710,000 gallons of No. 2 fuel oil.
Oil barge blows up, burns in NY-NJ area wracked by spills.

events.title: Buckeye Pipeline TransMix spill into Knapp Run and the Allegheny River near Freeport, Pennsylvania
events.description: A pipeline rupture attributed to a landslide released TransMix (mixed petroleum products) into Knapp Run and the Allegheny River near Freeport, Pennsylvania. Reports described downstream movement of the slick and precautionary actions affecting water intakes as response continued.

location.name: Knapp Run / Allegheny River near Freeport, Pennsylvania, USA

sources.headline: Fuel slick creeps down Allegheny River

key_quotes.quote: A landslide caused a pipeline rupture releasing TransMix into Knapp Run and the Allegheny River.
Incident 6744 documents the spill affecting the Allegheny River system.

events.title: Mid-March 1990 floods in Alabama from heavy rainfall
events.description: Heavy rainfall in mid-March 1990 led to significant river flooding across parts of Alabama, with record or near-record crests reported on multiple rivers and widespread impacts to roads and bridges. News reporting also described evacuations and flooding impacts in the region.

location.name: Alabama, USA

key_quotes.quote: Floods batter Alabama, South

events.title: Mid-March 1990 storm flooding in Alabama, Georgia, and Florida (USGS documented)
events.description: A cyclonic storm produced nearly continuous rainfall starting March 15, 1990 across southwestern Alabama and the Florida Panhandle and expanding into parts of Alabama and west-central Georgia on March 16. USGS documentation describes rainfall distribution and flood peaks at numerous gaging stations, indicating widespread flooding impacts across the three states.

location.name: United States (Alabama/Georgia/Florida)

sources.headline: Floods in February and March 1990 in Alabama, Georgia, and Florida (report PDF)
Floods in February and March 1990 in Alabama, Georgia, and Florida

key_quotes.quote: Floods in February and March 1990 in Alabama, Georgia, and Florida

events.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Vincent (Mar 2–4, 1990) in the Australian region
events.description: A tropical low in the monsoon trough intensified into Severe Tropical Cyclone Vincent during March 2–4, 1990 in the Australian region, peaking around March 4 before being affected by an approaching cold front.

location.name: Australia

key_quotes.quote: Severe Tropical Cyclone Vincent

events.title: Tropical Cyclone Hilda (Mar 4–7, 1990) in the Australian region
events.description: A low along the monsoonal trough developed into Tropical Cyclone Hilda from March 4–7, 1990 in the Australian region, reaching storm intensity around March 6 before weakening as it recurved southward in increasing wind shear.

location.name: Australia

key_quotes.quote: Tropical Cyclone Hilda

events.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Alex (Mar 15–26, 1990) in the Australian region
events.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Alex persisted from March 15–26, 1990 in the Australian region, intensifying to very strong winds and low central pressure before weakening. The Bureau of Meteorology history page also notes a reported trawler sinking associated with the event.

location.name: Australia

key_quotes.quote: Severe Tropical Cyclone Alex

events.title: M 7.6 earthquake south of the Fiji Islands
events.description: USGS reporting for March–April 1990 includes a major earthquake in the Fiji Islands region. The draft cites a widely indexed timestamp of 1990-03-03 12:16:27 UTC for an approximately M 7.6 event south of Fiji.

location.name: South of the Fiji Islands (South Pacific Ocean)

events.title: M 7.1 earthquake near Port-Vila, Shefa Province, Vanuatu
events.description: USGS reporting for March–April 1990 includes a major earthquake in the Vanuatu Islands region. The draft references an indexed event time of 1990-03-05 17:10:39 UTC for a significant earthquake near Port-Vila.

location.name: Vanuatu Islands region (near Port-Vila, Shefa, Vanuatu)

events.title: M 6.1 Kalat-area earthquake, Balochistan, Pakistan (damage reported)
events.description: The draft describes an earthquake near Kalat, Balochistan, Pakistan on 1990-03-04 19:46:19 UTC, with reports of deaths, injuries, and damage to homes. The provided sources include an indexed earthquake listing and USGS March–April 1990 reporting context.

location.name: Near Kalāt, Balochistān, Pakistan

events.title: M 6.4 earthquake southwest of Atka, Alaska (Aleutian Islands region)
events.description: USGS global event summaries for March 1990 list a strong earthquake in Alaska near Atka in the Aleutian Islands region. The draft provides only the UTC date (1990-03-12) without a specific origin time.

location.name: Near Atka, Aleutian Islands, Alaska, United States

events.title: Nicoya Gulf entrance earthquake, Costa Rica (Mw 7.0)
events.description: A large earthquake struck at the entrance to the Gulf of Nicoya, Costa Rica on 1990-03-25 at 13:22:55.6 UTC (reported as Mw 7.0 in the draft). The draft indicates considerable damage in central Costa Rica and a sequence of foreshocks and aftershocks documented by Costa Rican researchers.

location.name: Entrance to the Gulf of Nicoya, Costa Rica

dedupe.cluster_hint: 1990-03 Gulf of Nicoya Costa Rica earthquake

events.title: Wildfire burned ~1,920 ha in Phu Kadong National Park (Thailand)
events.description: A wildfire in early March 1990 burned about 1,920 hectares of pine forest and hill evergreen forest in Phu Kadong National Park. The source notes impacts due to the area’s watershed importance and tourism value.

location.name: Phu Kadong National Park, Loei Province, Thailand

key_quotes.quote: burned about 1,920 hectares of pine forest and hill evergreen forest

dedupe.cluster_hint: FAO Thailand national park wildfires March 1990

events.title: Wildfire burned ~480 ha in Doi Inthanon National Park (Thailand)
events.description: A mid-March 1990 wildfire burned about 480 hectares of dry forest and hill evergreen forest in Doi Inthanon National Park. The source reports impacts to a sensitive watershed area and cites about 20% mortality among mature trees.

location.name: Doi Inthanon National Park, Chiang Mai Province, Thailand

key_quotes.quote: burned about 480 hectares of dry forest and hill evergreen forest
mortality of about 20% of mature trees

dedupe.cluster_hint: FAO Thailand national park wildfires March 1990

events.title: Wildfire burned ~1,440 ha in Khao Yai National Park (Thailand)
events.description: A late-March 1990 wildfire burned about 1,440 hectares of dry evergreen forest in Khao Yai National Park. The source reports mature tree mortality and wildlife losses among small animals and birds.

location.name: Khao Yai National Park, Nakhon Ratchasima Province, Thailand

key_quotes.quote: burned about 1,440 hectares of dry evergreen forest

dedupe.cluster_hint: FAO Thailand national park wildfires March 1990

events.title: NPS Morning Report noted very high to extreme fire danger in parts of the U.S. (Mar 2, 1990)
events.description: The National Park Service Morning Report dated March 2, 1990 summarized recent wildland fire activity affecting multiple parks and reported very high to extreme fire danger conditions in parts of the United States, listing specific parks under those conditions.

location.name: United States (National Park Service Morning Report)

key_quotes.quote: Morning Report dated March 2, 1990 summarized February wildland fire activity

dedupe.cluster_hint: NPS Morning Report fire danger March 1990

events.title: U.S. Interior agencies sign MOU to expand wetlands work for waterfowl habitat
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the Bureau of Reclamation signed a memorandum of understanding to intensify Interior Department efforts to increase wetland breeding habitat for ducks, geese, and other waterfowl under the North American Waterfowl Management Plan.

location.name: United States (U.S. Department of the Interior)

sources.headline: Interior Secretary Lujan Announces Agreement to Intensify Department's Wetlands Efforts
Interior Secretary Lujan Announces Agreement to Intensify Department's Wetlands Efforts (PDF)

key_quotes.quote: signed a memorandum of understanding to intensify Interior’s efforts to increase wetland breeding habitat

dedupe.cluster_hint: US Interior wetlands MOU North American Waterfowl Management Plan (1990-03-15)

events.title: OSHA HAZWOPER standard (29 CFR 1910.120) reaches effective date
events.description: OSHA’s Hazardous Waste Operations and Emergency Response (HAZWOPER) standard reached its effective date on March 6, 1990. The standard sets worker safety and health requirements for hazardous waste operations and emergency response activities.

location.name: United States (federal standard)

key_quotes.quote: Effective date: March 6, 1990.

events.title: U.S. Senate agrees to Gore amendment on reducing ozone-depleting chemicals (S.Amdt. 1334)
events.description: The U.S. Senate agreed to the Gore amendment (S.Amdt. 1334) during consideration of Clean Air Act Amendments legislation (S. 1630) on March 8, 1990. The amendment concerned reduction of ozone-depleting chemicals.

location.name: United States Senate (Washington, DC)

sources.headline: U.S. Senate Roll Call Vote 33 (101st Congress, 2nd Session)

key_quotes.quote: On the Amendment (Gore Amendment No. 1334).

events.title: U.S. Senate agrees to Boschwitz amendment on state small business stationary source compliance assistance (S.Amdt. 1345)
events.description: The U.S. Senate agreed to the Boschwitz amendment (S.Amdt. 1345) during consideration of Clean Air Act Amendments legislation (S. 1630) on March 21, 1990. The amendment required states to adopt a small stationary source technical and environmental compliance assistance program.

location.name: United States Senate (Washington, DC)

sources.headline: U.S. Senate Roll Call Vote 36 (101st Congress, 2nd Session)

key_quotes.quote: On the Amendment (Boschwitz Amendment No. 1345).

events.title: U.S. Senate agrees to Adams amendment on visibility in national parks and wilderness areas (S.Amdt. 1354)
events.description: The U.S. Senate agreed to the Adams amendment (S.Amdt. 1354) during consideration of Clean Air Act Amendments legislation (S. 1630) on March 21, 1990. The amendment related to visibility protections in national parks and wilderness areas.

location.name: United States Senate (Washington, DC)

sources.headline: U.S. Senate Roll Call Vote 38 (101st Congress, 2nd Session)

key_quotes.quote: On the Amendment (Adams Amendment No. 1354).

events.title: EPA issues final rule revising RCRA toxicity characteristic (TCLP and added constituents)
events.description: EPA issued a final rule revising the RCRA toxicity characteristic used to identify certain wastes as hazardous. The rule replaced the EP leach test with the TCLP and added additional toxic constituents, with an effective date later in 1990.

location.name: United States (federal hazardous waste regulation)

sources.headline: Federal Register Notice March 29, 1990: Revisions to Toxicity Characteristic (Final Rule)
Hazardous Waste Management System; Identification and Listing of Hazardous Waste; Toxicity Characteristic Revisions (Final)

key_quotes.quote: Federal Register Notice March 29, 1990: Revisions to Toxicity Characteristic (Final Rule).
